question: Which compound noun means 'poison released by vehicle engines'?
Which collocation describes very high, fast-rising temperatures?
Which weather collocation means temperatures that are dangerously hot?
Read: 'By the time I get on the train next Saturday, I ________ this trip for two months!' (plan)
Read: 'When I arrive at the platform on Saturday, my friend Hamed ________ for me.' (wait)
Choose: 'By the end of the week, I hope we ________ Athens.' (reach)
Read: 'I think our plan ________ succeed.' (bound)
Which is the correct question form? 'Will Faten ________ school by 4 p.m.?'
Choose the correct option: 'Is Abeer ________ apologise for her behaviour?'
By the time they get home, Abbas and Jaber ________ for 18 hours.
Five years ________ now, Kamal will have gained his degree in astrophysics.
I hope that ________ the year 2050, doctors will have found a cure for cancer.
Which phrase means 'almost certainly will not happen'?
Read: 'It is essential that we all take immediate action to prevent this.' Which structure is 'it is essential that'?
choices: A formal email phrase for making a point forcefully
correct_answer: A formal email phrase for making a point forcefully

question: The final paragraph of a formal email should ________.
choices: ask the recipient to take action
correct_answer: ask the recipient to take action

question: In compound nouns where both words are nouns, the stress is on ________.
Which of these compound nouns has the stress on the SECOND word?
Which animal was assumed to be extinct until 2008?
What does the WWF use to raise money for critically endangered species?
choices: The image of the giant panda
correct_answer: The image of the giant panda

question: Read: 'I'm going to turn them into shorts.' This future form expresses ________.
'Send me the details — I'll take a look.' This expresses ________.
choices: a decision made at the moment of speaking
correct_answer: a decision made at the moment of speaking

question: Which option completes this sentence correctly? 'She ________ how to understand the labels on clothes.' (routine action — normal course of events)
Which phrase means 'expected to happen in the very near future'?
Read: 'I look forward to your response.' Where does this appear in a formal email?
Which of the following is a cause-and-effect phrase showing the cause comes AFTER the phrase?
Which phrase means 'to cause something to happen'?
Read: 'This may be due to better education.' Rewrite using 'result from': 'This may ________ better education.'
Which of these is NOT an example of expressing indecision?
choices: I can't make up my mind.
I'm on the fence about it.

question: Which phrase signals that the speaker is going to paraphrase what they just said?
choices: I'm going to be talking about

question: Engaging the audience in a presentation can be done by telling a ________ anecdote.
Read: 'I can't put my finger on it, but the one with the water bottle doesn't grab me.' What does the second part mean?
choices: It doesn't interest or appeal to me
correct_answer: It doesn't interest or appeal to me

question: Read: 'Okay, so shall we go with the one with the bright colours?' This phrase is used to ________.
Which adjective describes an animal that is pleasant to hug or hold?
Read: 'Driving is sure to become safer.' Which structure is this?
Choose: 'With such strong winds, the airport is ________ to be open.'
Read: 'We stopped the party due to the bad weather.' What caused the party to stop?
Choose the correct formal rewrite of 'I heard that sea plastic is harming over 600 species.'
choices: Sea plastic is estimated to be harming over 600 species.
I think sea plastic harms many species.
People say sea plastic harms species.
correct_answer: Sea plastic is estimated to be harming over 600 species.

question: The aye-aye is traditionally considered ________.
Which word describes an animal that prefers to live alone rather than in a group?
Read: 'In 100 years' time, half the world's languages will have disappeared.' This is an example of ________.
